1.texto_original: GIUBILA, o sterile, che non partorivi; fa’ risonar grida di allegrezza, e strilla, o tu, che non avevi dolori di parto; perciocchè i figliuoli della desolata saranno in maggior numero che quelli della maritata, ha detto il Signore.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Isaia 54:1

"GIUBILA, o sterile, che non partorivi; fa’ risonar grida di allegrezza, e strilla, o tu, che non avevi dolori di parto; perciocchè i figliuoli della desolata saranno in maggior numero che quelli della maritata, ha detto il Signore."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Isaia 54:1

Philological Analysis

"GIUBILA, o sterile, che non partorivi; fa’ risonar grida di allegrezza, e strilla, o tu, che non avevi dolori di parto; perciocchè i figliuoli della desolata saranno in maggior numero che quelli della maritata, ha detto il Signore."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"GIUBILA, o sterile, che non partorivi; fa’ risonar grida di allegrezza, e strilla, o tu, che non avevi dolori di parto; perciocchè i figliuoli della desolata saranno in maggior numero che quelli della maritata, ha detto il Signore."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

14.normal:
Devotional Study of Isaia 54:14

"Tu sarai stabilita in giustizia, tu sarai lontana d’oppressione, sì che non la temerai; e di ruina, sì ch’ella non si accosterà a te."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
14.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Isaia 54:14

Philological Analysis

"Tu sarai stabilita in giustizia, tu sarai lontana d’oppressione, sì che non la temerai; e di ruina, sì ch’ella non si accosterà a te."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
14.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Tu sarai stabilita in giustizia, tu sarai lontana d’oppressione, sì che non la temerai; e di ruina, sì ch’ella non si accosterà a te."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

15.texto_original: Ecco, ben si faranno delle raunate, ma non da parte mia; chi si radunerà contro a te, venendoti addosso, caderà.

16.texto_original: Ecco, io ho creato il fabbro che soffia il carbone nel fuoco, e che trae fuori lo strumento, per fare il suo lavoro; ed io ancora ho creato il guastatore, per distruggere.
16.normal:
Devotional Study of Isaia 54:16

"Ecco, io ho creato il fabbro che soffia il carbone nel fuoco, e che trae fuori lo strumento, per fare il suo lavoro; ed io ancora ho creato il guastatore, per distruggere."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

17.texto_original: Niun’arme fabbricata contro a te prospererà; e tu condannerai ogni lingua che si leverà contro a te in giudicio. Quest’è l’eredità de’ servitori del Signore, e la lor giustizia da parte mia, dice il Signore.
17.normal:
Devotional Study of Isaia 54:17

"Niun’arme fabbricata contro a te prospererà; e tu condannerai ogni lingua che si leverà contro a te in giudicio. Quest’è l’eredità de’ servitori del Signore, e la lor giustizia da parte mia, dice il Signore."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.
